items.d: to have bad luck — bad luck keeps happening to you.
items.s: Dan is always so ___ . Bad luck happens to him all the time.
items.hintDe: Wenn dir immer wieder etwas Schlechtes passiert.

items.d: to close your eyes and hope for a thing you really want.
items.s: Close your eyes and ___ on your birthday, then hope it comes true.
items.hintDe: Augen zu — und dir etwas wünschen.
items.note: Idea from SB task 4 sentence 2 ('If you see a rainbow, you can make a wish.'); 'see/rainbow/maybe' untaught → reset to a birthday-candles context with taught words.

items.d: to want a thing very much and to hope it happens.
items.s: A dream comes true if you ___ a bad thing, but no one does that.
items.hintDe: Sich etwas ganz fest wünschen.
items.note: Carrier from SB task 4 sentence 4 ('They come true if you wish for something bad.'); 'wishes/only/ever/something' untaught → singular 'A dream comes true if you ___ a bad thing'.

items.d: When people believe an object or action makes nice or unlucky things happen to you.
items.s: If you break a mirror, it ___ you seven years' bad luck, people think.
items.hintDe: Etwas sorgt dafür, dass du Glück oder Pech hast.
items.note: Carrier from SB task 4 sentence 1 ('If you break a mirror, it brings you seven years' bad luck.'); 'people think' added in-bank (bare 'say' is untaught).

items.d: when a dream or a hope really happens.
items.s: I really hope my dream of going to Japan will ___ one day.
items.hintDe: Wenn ein Wunsch oder Traum wirklich passiert.
items.note: Idea from SB Scene 4 ('did your dreams come true?'); carrier rebuilt with taught words ('see/rainbow' avoided).

items.d: scary and strange, like a place where ghosts live.
items.s: Wow, this home is really ___ . Who lives here?
items.hintDe: Ein bisschen gruselig — wie bei Geistern.
items.note: Carrier from SB Scene 3 ('this house is really spooky. Who lives here?'); 'house/old' untaught → 'this home'; 'strange' glossed in def.

items.d: When nice or unlucky things happen to you by chance, not by skill.
items.s: I ___ really good luck today, and I am very happy about it!
items.hintDe: Wenn dir im Leben Gutes oder Schlechtes widerfährt.
items.note: Lucky-find idea from v1 simplified ('found/five-euro note' untaught); 'chance' glossed in def (untaught).

items.d: to think that stories about luck and magic are true.
items.s: Do you really ___ , or do you think they are all just silly?
items.hintDe: Daran glauben, dass Geschichten über Glück wahr sind.
items.note: Idea from WB Dialogue 1 ('I don't believe in superstitions.'); 'silly' glossed in carrier (untaught).

items.d: a small machine next to your bed that makes a noise in the morning so you get up.
items.s: My ___ next to my bed helps me get up in the morning.
items.hintDe: Macht morgens Lärm, damit du aufstehst.
items.note: Idea from SB Scene 4 ('I really didn't want to get up when my alarm clock rang.'); 'rang/went off/woke/made' all untaught → 'helps me get up' with taught words.

items.d: a short way to ask if somebody did well.
items.s: Did you find your keys under the sofa? ___
items.hintDe: Kurze Frage: Hast du es geschafft / gefunden?
items.note: Carrier from SB Scene 5 ('Any luck?'); distractorPool keeps the answer in-set per gameMeta rule but mc excludes it.

items.d: very close to a thing or to somebody, next to them.
items.s: Mr Wallis, the teacher, arrives and sits down ___ them at the campfire.
items.hintDe: Direkt daneben.
items.note: Carrier from SB Scene 2 stage line ('Mr Wallis, the teacher, arrives and sits down beside them.'); 'campfire' glossed (untaught).

items.d: a nice way to ask if a thing is OK.
items.s: ___ if I open the window? It is very hot in here.
items.hintDe: Höflich fragen, ob etwas in Ordnung ist.
items.note: Idea from SB Scene 4 ('do you mind if I sit and join you?'); carrier rebuilt to avoid untaught 'sit'.

items.d: very bad and wanting to hurt people.
items.s: Candyman is an ___ spirit who wants to scare and hurt you.
items.hintDe: Sehr böse — will anderen wehtun.
items.note: Carrier from SB ('Candyman is an evil spirit.'); 'who wants to scare and hurt you' added in-bank to fix the blank.

items.d: a way to tell somebody you are not serious.
items.s: Don't worry, ___ ! I didn't really eat your last chocolate.
items.hintDe: Wenn du sagst, dass du es nicht ernst meinst.
items.note: Mined from v1 ('I didn't really eat your last chocolate bar').

items.d: to take no notice of somebody, like they are not there.
items.s: ___ him — he just wants to scare you.
items.hintDe: Jemanden gar nicht beachten.
items.note: Carrier from SB ('Ignore him – he's just being silly.'); 'being/silly' untaught → 'he just wants to scare you'.

items.d: happy and pleased with a thing because it is good.
items.s: I am not really ___ with your work — please do it again.
items.hintDe: Zufrieden, weil etwas gut genug ist.
items.note: Carrier from SB MORE Words example ('I am not really satisfied with your answer.'); 'answer/try' untaught → 'with your work — please do it again'.

items.d: to give a high cry because you are scared or hurt.
items.s: She wanted to ___ when the big black spider came near.
items.hintDe: Laut aufschreien, weil du Angst hast.
items.note: Idea from SB Scene 2 ('waking me up ... with your screams'); spider carrier mined from v1 with taught words.

items.d: a warm cover you lie inside when you go camping.
items.s: Nick is in his ___ , dreaming.
items.hintDe: Darin schläfst du warm beim Zelten.
items.note: Carrier from SB Scene 3 stage line ('Nick is lying in his sleeping bag.'); 'lying/tent' untaught, so 'is in ... dreaming'; 'warm' glossed in def.

items.d: a ghost or an evil thing — the dead man or woman who some people think still lives.
items.s: There is a ___ called Candyman who lives in this home.
items.hintDe: Ein Geist oder böses Wesen.
items.note: Carrier from SB ('There is a spirit by the name of Candyman.'); 'by the name of' (untaught 'name') swapped for 'called'; 'old house' (untaught 'old/house') → 'this home'.

items.d: a story about luck that many people think is true.
items.s: When you break a mirror, some people think it brings bad luck — but it's just a silly ___ .
items.hintDe: Eine Geschichte über Glück, die viele für wahr halten.
items.note: Carrier from SB ('It's only a silly superstition, after all.') reworked with the break-a-mirror idea; 'broke/things/only' untaught; 'silly' glossed (untaught).

items.d: to make a thing or animal come near to you.
items.s: If you whistle in the woods, you will ___ a spirit, the people in China think.
items.hintDe: Etwas oder jemanden näher zu dir bringen.
items.note: Carrier from SB ('If you whistle, you'll attract a spirit.'); 'you'll/old/says' untaught → 'you will ... the people in China think'; 'in the woods' added in-bank.

items.d: to go into a place.
items.s: When you want to ___ your home in China, you have to go this way or that way.
items.hintDe: In einen Raum oder ein Haus hineingehen.
items.note: Carrier from SB China text ('When you want to enter your home, you have to turn sharply to the left or right.'); 'turn/sharply/left/right' untaught → 'go this way or that way'.

items.d: when somebody makes your hair shorter for you.
items.s: Some people think that a new ___ before a big day at school brings bad luck.
items.hintDe: Wenn dir die Haare kürzer geschnitten werden.
items.note: Carrier from SB Vietnam text ('If you get a haircut before an exam...'); 'Vietnam/exam/forget' untaught → 'a big day at school'; def avoids untaught 'cut/hairdresser'.

items.d: so plain that everybody can understand it at once.
items.s: It was ___ that she was tired — she could not stop yawning in class.
items.hintDe: Ganz leicht zu sehen oder zu verstehen.
items.note: Idea from SB MORE Words example ('Isn't it obvious...'); v1 'tired/yawning' carrier reused; 'yawning' glossed (untaught).

items.d: done in this way for a very long time.
items.s: ___ homes in China have big spirit screens to keep ghosts out.
items.hintDe: Seit langer Zeit auf die gleiche Weise gemacht.
items.note: Carrier from SB China text ('Traditional homes in China have large spirit screens.'); 'large' (untaught) → 'big'; 'screens' glossed; 'to trick' clause reworded to 'keep ghosts out'.

items.d: to make somebody think a lie is true.
items.s: Some homes in China have spirit screens to ___ ghosts and stop them.
items.hintDe: Jemanden dazu bringen, eine Lüge zu glauben.
items.note: Carrier from SB China text ('Some homes in China have spirit screens to trick ghosts.'); 'and stop them' added in-bank.

items.d: having or bringing bad luck.
items.s: In Italy, the ___ number is 17, and many people stay away from it.
items.hintDe: Bringt Pech oder hat Pech.
items.note: Carrier from SB Italy text ('In Italy, the unlucky number is 17.'); 'and many people stay away from it' added in-bank.

items.d: to make a high sound with your mouth.
items.s: In Norway, people think that if you ___ when the sun shines, it will shine all afternoon.
items.hintDe: Mit dem Mund einen hohen Ton machen.
items.note: Carrier from SB Norway text ('If you whistle when the sun is shining, it will shine for more than two hours.'); 'say/hours' untaught → 'people think ... all afternoon'; 'lips' glossed in def (untaught).

items.d: a break in the ground or a wall, like a thin opening.
items.s: Some people think you will have bad luck if you go over a ___ in the pavement.
items.hintDe: Ein Spalt oder Riss im Boden oder in einer Wand.
items.note: Carrier from SB task 6 / grammar caption ('You'll have bad luck if you walk on the cracks in the pavement.'); 'walk/step/you'll' untaught → 'go over a crack'; 'thin' glossed in def.

items.d: a small grey forest animal of the woods that calls in spring.
items.s: We could hear a ___ calling over and over from the trees in the woods.
items.hintDe: Grauer Waldvogel, der im Frühling Kuckuck ruft.
items.note: Carrier from SB MORE Words example ('I can hear a cuckoo in the woods.') expanded; 'hear' glossed (untaught); def avoids untaught 'bird/says'.

items.d: the place beside a road where people go on foot.
items.s: Stay on the ___ next to the buildings, not on the road.
items.hintDe: Der harte Weg neben der Straße zum Gehen.
items.note: Idea from SB MORE Words example ('I sometimes don't walk on the cracks in the pavement.'); 'walk/busy/safer/hard/part' untaught → 'Stay on the ___ next to the buildings, not on the road.'

items.d: having a lot of money.
items.s: You will be ___ if you hear a cuckoo and shake the money in your pocket.
items.hintDe: Wenn du sehr viel Geld hast.
items.note: Carrier from SB task 6 ('You'll get rich if you hear a cuckoo and shake your money.'); 'you'll/get' untaught → 'You will be ___'; 'hear' glossed (untaught).

items.d: to make a thing go up and down fast many times.
items.s: I always ___ the money in my pocket if I hear a cuckoo.
items.hintDe: Etwas schnell auf und ab bewegen.
items.note: Carrier from SB MORE Words example ('I always shake the money in my pocket if I hear a cuckoo.'); 'hear' glossed (untaught); def avoids untaught 'move/hand/side'.

items.d: feeling that stories about luck and magic are true.
items.s: My grandmother is very ___ , so she will never break a mirror.
items.hintDe: Wenn jemand an Glücks- und Pechgeschichten glaubt.
items.note: Idea from SB ('Are you superstitious?') + WB Carla story ('Breaking a mirror brings ... bad luck'); 'walks/ladder' untaught → 'will never break a mirror'; 'magic' glossed in def (untaught).

items.d: to become ill, with a runny nose and a cough.
items.s: Wear a warm jacket in the snow, or you will ___ and be sick in bed.
items.hintDe: Krank werden mit Schnupfen und Husten.
items.note: Idea from SB EXTRA example ('If you don't wear a jacket, you'll catch a cold.'); 'you'll' → 'you will'; 'runny/cough/warm' glossed in def/carrier (untaught).

items.d: a small brush to clean your teeth.
items.s: If you drop your ___ on the ground, you have to wash it first.
items.hintDe: Damit putzt du dir die Zähne.
items.note: Carrier from SB EXTRA example ('If you drop your toothbrush, you'll have to wash it.'); 'you'll/floor/use' untaught → 'on the ground, you have to wash it first'.

items.d: to agree on a time and place to do a thing.
items.s: Can we ___ a time to meet after school on Friday?
items.hintDe: Eine Zeit und einen Ort zum Treffen ausmachen.
items.note: Idea from SB dialogue ('Have you got something else arranged?'); 'something/else/next' untaught → 'Can we ___ a time to meet after school on Friday?'

items.d: a way to tell somebody you are really certain about a thing.
items.s: Will the train come on time? — ___ It is always on time.
items.hintDe: Wenn du dir bei etwas ganz sicher bist.
items.note: Mined from v1 (train-on-time idea); 'certain' glossed in def (untaught).

items.d: a small object you carry because you think it brings you good luck.
items.s: She always carries her ___ , a small horseshoe, in her pocket every day for good luck.
items.hintDe: Ein kleiner Gegenstand, der dir Glück bringen soll.
items.note: Mined from v1 (horseshoe-in-pocket idea); 'tiny/silver/test' untaught → 'a small horseshoe ... every day for good luck'; 'object' and 'horseshoe' glossed (untaught).

items.d: white, and you put it on food to give it more taste.
items.s: I dropped the ___ on the table — some people think that is bad luck.
items.hintDe: Weißes Streugut, das Essen würzt.
items.note: Idea from SB EXTRA example ('Oh no, I spilled the salt on the table.') + WB Carla; 'spilled/oh/powder/stuff' untaught → 'I dropped the ___ on the table'; distractors are in-bank nouns.

items.d: really, not for fun.
items.s: Do you ___ believe in all of these stories? They are just stories!
items.hintDe: Wirklich, nicht als Spaß gemeint.
items.note: Carrier from SB EXTRA example ('You seriously believe in all these things?'); 'believe' glossed (untaught as bare word); 'They are just stories!' added in-bank.
